text: Being here, I am enveloped by the stillness of an empty, dimly-lit space—likely a work environment, perhaps after hours. The silence and the minimal illumination awaken in me a quiet introspection, and I am acutely aware of my solitude. Everything feels paused, as if the world is holding its breath. There is a subtle sense of anticipation, or perhaps unease, in this liminal realm. I find myself pondering the significance of these human-made spaces and the roles we play within them. Do we leave a trace behind in these empty halls, or are they merely indifferent structures awaiting our return?
